ddls:
CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "department_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department manager */,
  "department_created_date" TEXT /* Date the department was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_roles" (
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job role */,
  "job_role_name" TEXT /* Name of the job role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job role */,
  "job_role_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job role manager */,
  "job_role_created_date" TEXT /* Date the job role was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee receiving feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date the feedback was given */,
  "feedback_rating" TEXT /* Rating of the feedback (e.g., L for low, M for medium, H for high) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments from the manager */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager providing feedback */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance review */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being reviewed */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was conducted */,
  "review_score" INTEGER /* Score given in the performance review */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reviewer (manager) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compensation" (
  "compensation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compensation entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "monthly_income" REAL /* Monthly income of the employee */,
  "monthly_rate" REAL /* Monthly rate of the employee */,
  "stock_option_level" INTEGER /* Level of stock options */,
  "percent_salary_hike" INTEGER /* Percentage salary hike */,
  "bonus" REAL /* Bonus amount */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compensation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "training" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee attending the training */,
  "training_name" TEXT /* Name of the training program */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date the training was conducted */,
  "training_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the training in hours */,
  "training_type" TEXT /* Type of training (e.g., in-person, online) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_history" (
  "job_history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job history entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "previous_job_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the previous job role */,
  "previous_department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the previous department */,
  "promotion_date" TEXT /* Date of the job change or promotion */,
  "reason_for_change" TEXT /* Reason for the job change or promotion */,
  "job_change_type" TEXT /* Type of job change (e.g., promotion, transfer, demotion) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_history_previous_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("previous_job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_history_previous_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("previous_department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "satisfaction" (
  "satisfaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each satisfaction entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "environment_satisfaction" INTEGER /* Satisfaction with the work environment */,
  "relationship_satisfaction" INTEGER /* Satisfaction with relationships at work */,
  "job_involvement" INTEGER /* Level of job involvement */,
  "job_satisfaction" INTEGER /* Level of job satisfaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("satisfaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Name of the manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the manager */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the manager belongs to */,
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job role of the manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_managers_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_managers_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id")
)
